metadata.title: Roseline Abara: The Ghost Artist Who Sings for Every Child

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A ghost artist is a defined character who generates new stories rather than reciting songs she was taught. Her identity is fixed by answering what she sounds like and who she sings to. She was built to serve the Lyrical Literacy mission of reaching every child through song. The persona itself, not a fixed playlist, is what makes her outputs coherent and reusable.

artifactLines: A ghost artist is a defined character who generates new stories rather than reciting songs she was taught.
Her identity is fixed by answering what she sounds like and who she sings to.
She was built to serve the Lyrical Literacy mission of reaching every child through song.
The persona itself, not a fixed playlist, is what makes her outputs coherent and reusable.

beats.narration_text: The idea that the character generates the story, not the songs she was taught, is what stuck with me. I want to actually build a small ghost artist for a literacy project. Help me define one. Give me: (1) a short persona spec covering how she sounds and who she sings to, (2) the pipeline steps that turn that persona into a generated song, (3) one concrete Lyrical Literacy use case with a sample prompt I could run today.
